R Notebook

# Core packages for manipulation and plotting
#install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, readxl, brms, emmeans, gghalves, install = TRUE, update = TRUE) 

trying URL 'https://cran.rstudio.com/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/contrib/4.5/checkmate_2.3.2.tgz'
trying URL 'https://cran.rstudio.com/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/contrib/4.5/insight_1.3.1.tgz'
trying URL 'https://cran.rstudio.com/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/contrib/4.5/V8_6.0.5.tgz'
trying URL 'https://cran.rstudio.com/bin/macosx/big-sur-arm64/contrib/4.5/xfun_0.52.tgz'

The downloaded binary packages are in
    /var/folders/mt/pg064h896b55fxxlh7bxd76hl_sw0r/T//RtmpYdkcZv/downloaded_packages
df<- read_xlsx('./water_samples.xlsx') |> 
  mutate(exp = ifelse(str_starts(`Sample Description`, "t"), "j", "e"),
         amitriptyline = as.numeric(ifelse(str_starts(Amitriptyline, "<"), "0", Amitriptyline)),
         id = `Sample Description`) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!str_starts(`Sample No.`, "D"),
                exp =='j') |> 
  select(-c(exp, `Sample Description`))

df$treatment <- factor(df$treatment, levels = c("control", "low", "high"))

summary(df)
  Reference         Description         Sample No.         Replicate         Date Sampled       Type of Sample     Amitriptyline      Alpha-Trenbolone     treatment       day   
 Length:89          Length:89          Length:89          Length:89          Length:89          Length:89          Length:89          Length:89          control:17   Min.   :1  
 Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   Class :character   low    :36   1st Qu.:1  
 Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Mode  :character   high   :36   Median :2  
                                                                                                                                                                      Mean   :2  
                                                                                                                                                                      3rd Qu.:3  
                                                                                                                                                                      Max.   :3  
 amitriptyline       id           
 Min.   :   0   Length:89         
 1st Qu.:  20   Class :character  
 Median :  74   Mode  :character  
 Mean   : 222                     
 3rd Qu.: 370                     
 Max.   :1200                     
fig1 <- ggplot(df, aes(x = treatment, y = amitriptyline, fill = treatment)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 30, color = "#FFBB7D")+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 300, color = "#EA682F") +
  geom_half_boxplot(errorbar.draw = F) +
  geom_half_point(alpha = 0.3)+
  ylab("Concentration of amitriptyline (ng/L)") +
  xlab("Treatment group") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("control" = "grey2", "low" = "#FFBB7D", "high" = "#EA682F")) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

fig1

formula <- bf(
  amitriptyline ~ treatment + s(hours, by = treatment, k = 3),
  sigma ~ treatment + s(hours, by = treatment, k = 3),
  family = gaussian()
)
model <- brm(
  formula = formula,
  data = model_df,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95, max_treedepth = 12),
  iter = 6000,
  warmup = 1000,
  threads = threading(2),
  chains = 4,
  cores = 4,
  backend = 'cmdstanr',
  seed = 1
)
G3;Compiling Stan program...
g

summary(model)

G2;H2;Warningh: There were 3925 divergent transitions after warmup. Increasing adapt_delta above 0.95 may help. See http://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmupg
 Family: gaussian 
  Links: mu = identity; sigma = log 
Formula: amitriptyline ~ treatment + s(hours, by = treatment, k = 3) 
         sigma ~ treatment + s(hours, by = treatment, k = 3)
   Data: model_df (Number of observations: 72) 
  Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 6000; warmup = 1000; thin = 1;
         total post-warmup draws = 20000

Smoothing Spline Hyperparameters:
                                 Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
sds(shourstreatmentlow_1)          135.66    137.17     8.32   465.65 1.00     1461     3197
sds(shourstreatmenthigh_1)         186.33    181.45     7.85   642.13 1.00     4054     6251
sds(sigma_shourstreatmentlow_1)     45.64     66.24     0.70   224.41 1.00     1085      947
sds(sigma_shourstreatmenthigh_1)    39.04     67.26     0.22   236.87 1.01     1512      630

Regression Coefficients:
                             Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
Intercept                       51.14      5.31    40.61    61.42 1.00     1211     1149
sigma_Intercept                  3.26      0.13     3.01     3.53 1.01     1354      454
treatmenthigh                  432.68     43.18   349.85   518.22 1.00     3950     7205
sigma_treatmenthigh              2.25      0.18     1.89     2.60 1.00     2260     8226
shours:treatmentlow_1          -19.08      5.93   -30.81    -7.03 1.00     5141     5456
shours:treatmenthigh_1         -79.58     46.25  -172.68     9.17 1.00     2158     5805
sigma_shours:treatmentlow_1     -0.28      0.12    -0.52    -0.03 1.00     7896     5258
sigma_shours:treatmenthigh_1    -0.03      0.13    -0.28     0.21 1.00     4065     6679

Draws were sampled using sample(hmc). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
plot(model, ask = FALSE)

pp_check(model)
G3;Using 10 posterior draws for ppc type 'dens_overlay' by default.
g

conditional_effects(model)

G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g

period <- 120  # hours per redose cycle

model_df <- model_df |>
  mutate(
    hours = as.numeric(hours),
    # wrap onto [0, period); 120 becomes 0, 192 -> 72, etc.
    hours_cycle = (hours %% period)
  )
formula <- bf(
  amitriptyline ~ treatment +     
    s(hours_cycle,
      by = treatment,
      k = 2,
      bs = "cc"
    ),

  sigma ~ treatment +
    s(hours_cycle,
      by = treatment,
      k = 2,
      bs = "cc"
    ),
  family = gaussian()
)
model <- brm(
  formula = formula,
  data = model_df,
  knots = list(hours_cycle = c(0, period)), 
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95, max_treedepth = 12),
  iter = 6000, warmup = 1000,
  threads = threading(2),
  chains = 4, cores = 4,
  backend = "cmdstanr",
  seed = 1
)
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
G2;H2;Warningh in smooth.construct.cc.smooth.spec(object, dk$data, dk$knots) :
  basis dimension, k, increased to minimum possible
g
